title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq 100 Rallies, Oil Sinks 5% As U.S.-Iran Deal Reopens Hormuz
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stocks rallied sharply on Monday following a U.S.-Iran peace agreement that reopens the Strait of Hormuz. Crude oil tumbled 5%, easing inflation fears and powering gains in technology stocks. The Nasdaq 100 surged 2.9%, S&P 500 climbed 1.8%, and the Dow advanced 1.3%. Energy stocks declined while chip makers and gold miners soared.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Jumped 5.8% as technology sector rallied on lower inflation expectations from oil price collapse

title: Chemical Mechanical Planarization Market to Hit $13.23 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global Chemical Mechanical Planarization (CMP) market, valued at USD 6.65 billion in 2025, is expected to grow at a CAGR of 7.12% through 2035, driven by semiconductor investments, 5G adoption, and advanced manufacturing initiatives. North America leads with 40% market share, while Asia Pacific shows fastest growth at 10.85% CAGR. CMP equipment dominates with 60% revenue share, though consumables segment grows fastest.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as leading market player in growing CMP equipment segment which dominates with 60% revenue share; benefits from increasing semiconductor manufacturing investments and technological advancement
Leading market player positioned in growing CMP consumables segment, which is fastest-growing segment with increasing demand for slurries and polishing pads
Recognized by Samsung Electronics for pioneering CMP polishing pads enabling next-gen semiconductors; recent innovation recognition supports market position
Recently announced 4 billion yen investment to expand CMP slurries production in Belgium, demonstrating confidence in market growth and European semiconductor expansion

title: Entegris Taps Mike Sauer As Interim CFO, Reaffirms Outlook
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Entegris announced that CFO Linda LaGorga will step down effective February 28, 2026, with Mike Sauer, the VP and Controller, taking over as Interim CFO on March 1, 2026. The company reaffirmed its Q4 2025 guidance with projected sales of $790-$830 million and adjusted EPS of 62-69 cents. The departure was stated to be by mutual agreement and unrelated to any operational or accounting disagreements.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the CFO departure could raise concerns about leadership stability, the company's reaffirmation of guidance, smooth transition plan with LaGorga staying as advisor, and positive commentary about Sauer's qualifications mitigate negative sentiment. The stock declined 1.30% on the news, reflecting modest market concern, but the overall messaging is reassuring.

title: Nike, Insmed, And Arm Are Among Top 10 Large Cap Losers Last Week (Dec. 15-Dec. 19): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks experienced significant declines last week. Nike fell 12.81% due to a 17% decline in Greater China sales and analyst downgrades. ARM Holdings dropped 9.77% following Goldman Sachs and Bank of America downgrades. Other major losers included Lennar (9.94% decline after earnings miss), Marathon Petroleum (9.42%), Phillips 66 (8.84%), Coupang (8.52%), BitMine Immersion (9.15%), Insmed (11.37%), Entegris (5.90%), and ServiceNow (0.78%).

title: Wafer Cleaning Equipment Market Size to Hit $15.61 Billion by 2033 | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The wafer cleaning equipment market is projected to grow from $10.23 billion in 2025 to $15.61 billion by 2033, driven by advanced semiconductor manufacturing needs and increasing demand for high-performance electronic devices.
